nameMeanings.name: Fulmer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Fulmer in Buckinghamshire or Fowlmere in Cambridgeshire, so named from Old English fugol ‘bird’ + mere ‘lake’.
Americanized form of German   Vollmer   and   Follmer  . Compare   Folmer   and   Fullmer  .

variants.name: Fulmore
meanings: Possibly an altered form of   Fulmer  . Compare   Fullmer  .

variants.name: Vollmer
meanings: German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements folk ‘people’ + meri, mari ‘famous’. This surname is also found in France (mainly Alsace). Compare Follmer , Fullmer , and Volmer .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Fulmer was born in 1297, in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son with Margery. He died in 1389, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 92.
